textPlain: This is the part in the movie where they have to convince the grizzled hacker to come out of retirement because he's the only one who can actually operate Emacs or vim and write code.
On flights with shitty wifi I have been running gpt-oss:120b on my macbook using ollama. Ok model for coding if you can't reach a good one.
The monster babbleth no more, sire.
> This incident has been resolved.
It reminds me of early day of Twitter's fail whale.

This is why I asked this question yesterday:"Ask HN: Why don't programming language foundations offer "smol" models?"https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=45840078If I could run smol single language models myself, I would not have to worry.
everyone, switch to open ai for 50% off. today only!
Yeah, getting this;API Error: 529 {"type":"error","error":{"type":"overloaded_error","message":"Overloaded"},"request_id":null}
It's over!
Better start catching up with latest developments on HN
what are we gonna dooo?
[dead]
Sir the vibe coding didn’t work, break the glass and call in dev!
That must be a beefed up MacBook (or you must be quite patient).gpt-oss:20b on my M1 MBP is usable but quite slow.
Could you share which Macbook model? And what context size you're getting.

GPT-OSS-120b/20b is probably the best you can run on your own hardware today. Be careful with the quantized versions though, as they're really horrible compared to the native MXFP4. I haven't looked in this particular case, but Ollama tends to hide their quantizations for some reason, so most people who could be running 20B with MXFP4, are still on Q8 and getting much worse results than they could.
Are you running the full 65GB model on a MacBook Pro? What tokens per second do you get? What specs? M5?

> I wonder why I can't find a model that only does Python and is good only at thatI don't think it's that easy. The times I've trained my own tiny models on just one language (programming or otherwise), they tend to get worse results than the models I've trained where I've chucked in all the languages I had at hand, even when testing just for single languages.It seems somewhat intuitive to me that it works like that too, programming in different (mainstream) languages is more similar than it's different (especially when 90% of all the source code is Algol-like), so makes sense there is a lot of cross-learning across languages.
because a smol model that any of the nonprofits could feasibly afford to train would be useless for actual code generation.Hell, even the huge foundational models are still useless in most scenarios.
The answer to most convenient solutions is money. There's no money in that.
Have you even tried Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B?
OpenAI's track record has been rather poor this month as well actually, look at all the yellows and reds: https://status.openai.com/
(in Homestar Runner voice) The good times awe ovew!
